front: Film: set in the fictional Republic of Zubrowka, based on Stefan Zweig stories; Ralph Fiennes plays concierge Gustave H and Zero Moustafa is his bellhop
Film: the plot revolves around the painting 'Boy with Apple' bequeathed by Madame D; Deputy Kovacs (Jeff Goldblum) is found stuffed in a sarcophagus with four fingers missing
Film: items are smuggled in pink Mendl's pastry boxes; graphic designer Annie Atkins misspelled 'patisserie,' requiring VFX changes to 3,000 boxes
Film: features a first-person tandem sled chase from a monastery to a luge track; the interior set was built in a mall lobby in Görlitz
Film: 12-year-old Khaki Scout Sam Shakusky runs away with Suzy Bishop; they met during a performance of Benjamin Britten's Noye's Fludde
Film: Cousin Ben (Jason Schwartzman) officiates the protagonists' wedding, complaining about $75 in nickels; Captain Sharp (Bruce Willis) later adopts Sam
Film: Steve Zissou (Bill Murray) seeks revenge on a jaguar shark; inspired by Jacques Cousteau; Brazilian musician Seu Jorge performs Portuguese David Bowie covers throughout
back: The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou (Wes Anderson)
work: The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou (2004)

front: Film: Frances McDormand plays a journalist who helps Timothée Chalamet's teenage revolutionary write his manifesto, which he obsesses over in the bath ('Revisions to a Manifesto' / 'Chessboard Revolution' segment)
Film: debut feature starring brothers Luke and Owen Wilson; protagonist robs a bookstore with Dignan and pursues a Paraguayan maid at a Texas motel

Director: signature style uses flat symmetric 'planimetric' shots with vibrant pastel colors; consistently uses Futura typeface; frequent collaborators include Owen Wilson, Jason Schwartzman, Adrien Brody, and Tilda Swinton
